name: Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop
name_original: Salto de la Minyona - circular gravel por el Espacio Natural de Les Guilleries-Savassona
description: This difficult 29.7-mile touring cycling route combines the Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop, offering diverse terrain and reservoir views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Les Masies De Voltregà navigate a rural municipality in Catalonia, characterized by diverse landscapes. The area features agroforestry landscapes with oak and holm oak forests, alongside the meandering Ter River and its protected riparian forests. Cyclists encounter varied topography, ranging from flatter plains in the northwest of the Plana de Vic to undulating terrain leading towards the Lluçanès foothills. This blend of natural features provides a rich setting for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Les Masies De Voltregà The most popular touring cycling route is Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop, a 29.7 miles (47.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route explores the Ter River's riparian forests and the Savassona area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Trona Viewpoint – Sant Bartomeu del Grau Church loop from Torelló, a difficult 41.6 miles (66.9 km) path. This challenging path offers views towards La Trona Viewpoint and passes the Sant Bartomeu del Grau Church. Local touring cyclists also love the Start of the Bellmunt Ascent – Bellmunt Sanctuary loop from Torelló, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail leading through initial foothills towards the Bellmunt Sanctuary, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Les Masies De Voltregà is defined by rural landscapes, Ter River riparian forests, and undulating terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flatter plains to more challenging ascents. The routes in Les Masies De Voltregà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 5,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Les Masies De Voltregà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: There are over 350 touring cycling routes around Les Masies De Voltregà, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: What are the typical terrain and elevation like for touring cycling in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: The terrain in Les Masies De Voltregà is quite varied. You'll find a mix of flatter plains in the northwest of the Plana de Vic, especially along the Ter River, and more undulating terrain as you head towards the Lluçanès foothills. Routes can range from gentle riverside paths to challenging ascents, such as those leading towards the Bellmunt Sanctuary.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: The routes cater to various skill levels. There are 26 easy routes, 118 moderate routes, and 206 difficult routes available. This means you can find everything from relaxed rides through rural landscapes to more challenging climbs with significant elevation gains.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: Les Masies de Voltregà is suitable for cycling throughout much of the year. The region experiences a thermal inversion, leading to 'fog forests' in the plain, which can create unique atmospheric conditions. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, while summer can be warm, and winter might be cooler, especially in higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Les Masies De Voltregà are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976054533/ruta-del-ter-and-savassona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that explores the Ter River's riparian forests. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19202929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trona Viewpoint – Sant Bartomeu del Grau Church loop from Torelló&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: You'll encounter diverse natural beauty, including extensive agroforestry landscapes with typical oak and holm oak forests. The meandering Ter River forms the eastern boundary, offering protected riparian forests that are excellent for birdwatching. The varied topography also means you'll experience both flat plains and undulating hills.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in cultural heritage. You can visit the impressive Baroque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2261304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellmunt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, small Romanesque churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2815824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí Xic Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, or the ruins of Voltregà Castle. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Feliuet de Savassona Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra de les Bruixes Rock Engravings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which routes offer the best viewpoints?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend towards higher ground. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Start of the Bellmunt Ascent – Bellmunt Sanctuary loop from Torelló&lt;/a&gt; leads to the Bellmunt Sanctuary, which offers expansive views of the Plain of Vic and the Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19202929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trona Viewpoint – Sant Bartomeu del Grau Church loop from Torelló&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent vistas.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads for touring cyclists?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Les Masies De Voltregà is a rural municipality with scattered nuclei and farmhouses. It's generally possible to find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many routes. We recommend checking the tour details on komoot for specific parking suggestions for your chosen route.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: Yes, with 26 easy routes available, there are options suitable for families. The flatter sections along the Ter River or through the rural plains would be ideal for a more relaxed family outing. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances for a comfortable experience with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: Many outdoor areas in Les Masies De Voltregà, especially the rural and forest paths, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes or protected areas. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Les Masies De Voltregà?
A: The touring cycling routes in Les Masies De Voltregà are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 5,400 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Ter River, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks.

Religious sanctuary located in the main square of Folgueroles (Osona) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.
The current temple has a single nave with a semicircular apse. Originally it appears to have had three apses, two of which were demolished in the 17th century

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for the Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop?
faqs.answer: Given that this route combines sections of the Ruta del Ter and the Savassona area, a touring bicycle might find some parts challenging. The Savassona area is primarily designed for mountain biking, with unpaved and difficult segments. While gravel bikes could handle some rougher tracks, a touring bike with narrow tires and less robust suspension might struggle. For a smoother experience, consider a gravel bike or a mountain bike, especially for the Savassona portion.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' for touring bicycles. The combination of the Ruta del Ter, which has challenging mountainous sections, and the Savassona area, known for mountain biking terrain, means it's not ideal for beginners. Some parts may require significant fitness, advanced riding skills, or even dismounting and walking your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter diverse scenery and cultural highlights. Notable points include the stunning Salto de la Miñona viewpoint, offering picturesque views. You'll also pass by the historic Church of Santa María de Folgueroles. The route also includes sections of the Ronda de Gurb Route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly diverse and can be challenging for a touring bicycle. Sections of the Ruta del Ter can involve rougher tracks, especially in the initial stages, and some parts may require dismounting. The Savassona area features primarily unpaved segments designed for mountain biking, ranging from easy trails to hard rides with significant elevation. Expect a mix of small roads, gravel tracks, and cycleways, with some potentially difficult unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop?
faqs.answer: Parking options are available in towns along the Ruta del Ter, such as Folgueroles, which is near the starting point of this specific loop. For the Savassona Natural Park area, there are designated parking zones, but it's advisable to check local signage for specific regulations and availability, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer milder temperatures and more stable weather conditions, making the diverse terrain more enjoyable. Summer can be very hot, especially in the lower sections, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse conditions in higher or more exposed areas.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ruta del Ter and Savassona Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trails within the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona and along the Ruta del Ter. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on the public trails within the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and be aware that certain activities or events might require special authorization. It's always a good idea to check the park's official website for any updates before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant portions of other established paths. You will find yourself on sections of the Ruta del Ter (45% of the route), Senders del 1714 - Etapa 06 (34%), and TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus (34%). Other notable intersecting trails include El Camí IT7TR4 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses (23%) and Folgueroles-Salt de la Minyona-Tavèrnoles (15%).

faqs.question: Are there places to find food and drink along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through varied landscapes, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections. Towns and villages along the Ruta del Ter, such as Folgueroles, will have cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can resupply. In the more natural areas of Savassona, amenities will be scarce, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, pack essentials like a repair kit, spare tubes, a pump, and basic tools. Carry enough water and snacks, as amenities can be sparse. Sunscreen, a hat, and appropriate layers of clothing are recommended for changing weather. A first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a GPS device or a fully charged phone with offline maps), and a headlamp are also advisable.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Folgueroles
